title: Mass Assignment / Over-Posting
principle: Bind only the fields a user is allowed to set. Passing a whole request body straight into a model or ORM create/update lets an attacker set fields you never exposed — is_admin, role, account_id. Use an explicit allowlist (strong parameters, a DTO, or serializer fields) and copy just those.

snippets.note: permit lists exactly the settable attributes; anything else (like :admin) is dropped. Never pass raw params to create/update/assign_attributes.

snippets.library: an explicit field allowlist (or a DRF serializer / WTForms)
snippets.vulnerable: user = User(**request.json)   # sets every posted field
snippets.note: With Django REST Framework, list fields explicitly on the serializer (avoid fields = '__all__' for writes). With SQLAlchemy, assign only allowlisted attributes.

snippets.library: a dedicated DTO with only the bindable fields
snippets.note: Bind to a request DTO that exposes only user-settable fields, then copy into the entity. Or use @JsonIgnore / a Jackson mixin to block sensitive fields on the entity.
Bind to a view model, or use [Bind(...)] to allowlist properties. Never model-bind an EF entity that carries fields like IsAdmin directly from the request.

references.title: CWE-915: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes
